title: Podman rootless on different volume (and config weird behavior)
body:
### Issue Description

Your "top of Podman's README.md" link above is broken. PM info is below.

I'm trying to use Podman in rootless mode and getting "Error: chmod /media/user: operation not permitted". I've set the graphroot and rootless_storage_path in /home/user/.config/containers/storage.conf (see below). Here's the weird thing.. when I change the runRoot in this file and check `podman info` nothing happens. But when I then change the graphRoot and check, the runRoot is changed. Even weirder, when I change the graphRoot back, it changes the runRoot back by itself. 

Anyone can explain this? Thx

This might be a bug, but my main issue is overcoming the chmod error (presumably from having the container on a different volume, because running with root works). I'm trying to run in rootless mode, so likely this is a permissions error. The ownership and permissions of the directories match my user though. If I use a directory with root ownership, I get an error, which makes sense. I don't know why PM would need to chmod /media/user which needs to be root ownership.

EDIT: Okay, so switching the graphRoot and rootless path to my home dir, resolves the error. And oddly enough, allowed me to change the runRoot to my user (this still could be consistent with a bug, unless PM checks the volume ownerships and guesses the best runRoot). I'll try with a symlink from my home dir.

EDIT2: symlinking failed with the same error. I tried chmoding the dir with recursive 777, with no success. Here is the stat /media/user/temp/containers output: 
`Access: (2777/drwxrwsrwx)  Uid: ( 1000/    user)   Gid: ( 1000/    user)`

--

    [storage]
    
    # Default Storage Driver, Must be set for proper operation.
    #driver = "overlay"
    driver = "vfs"
    
    # Temporary storage location
    #runroot = "/media/user/temp/containers/run"
    runroot = "/run/user/1000"
    
    # Primary Read/Write location of container storage
    graphroot = "/media/user/temp/containers/storage"
    
    # Storage path for rootless users
    rootless_storage_path = "/media/user/temp/containers/storage"

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

comments.body: podman caches the paths in its db, you need to run `podman system reset` before changing the paths.
The db is stored in the graphroot so when you change that it will not see the old paths anymore and start from scratch basically.

> podman caches the paths in its db, you need to run `podman system reset` before changing the paths.

Thanks for the info on the db being tied to graphroot. 

If I use `podman system reset` it will delete my downloaded containers.

I thought having to destroy everything to reset a conf file seemed ridiculous, and it was. Just rename the file and run `podman info` again. 

Also, when using symlinks the info output automatically changes to the real paths. Unlinking changes them back.
